plainEnglish: At Level AAA, every pre-recorded video-with-audio (and every silent video) provides a full text alternative—a document describing all the dialogue, sounds, and visual action in order. This serves people who are both deaf and blind (reading with a braille display) as well as anyone who prefers reading.
whyItMatters: Deafblind users who rely on a braille display can only access your video through a complete text alternative.
quickCheck: Confirm a full text alternative document—dialogue plus visual description—is linked near the video.
commonMistakes: Providing captions and audio description but no single readable text document.
Writing a transcript that lists dialogue but omits the visual scene descriptions.
Putting the transcript behind a download instead of a readable, linkable page.

codeExamples.label: Video with a linked full text alternative
Video with no full text alternative
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/keynote.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/keynote.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
</video>
